page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance includes 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage as instance store volumes and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations show average multi-core CPU performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 34594.3 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11582.0. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 25%. However, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specific areas, achieving a top 25% rating in Passmark CPU encryption and Passmark memory benchmarks. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform within the middle 50% tier.

The c5ad.8xlarge offers a balanced cost-efficiency profile for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from high physical core density and local NVMe storage without requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios. The inclusion of 1200 GB of fast local SSD storage makes it suitable for workloads requiring temporary scratch space, caching, or high-speed local data processing. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and database operations that can leverage the dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network speed of 10 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, with strong results in CPU encryption and memory performance, though single-core processing represents a performance tradeoff. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy applications requiring fast local storage. It is well-suited for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage on AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R32 processor at 3.3 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in CPU encryption and memory benchmarks
Tradeoff of lower single-core CPU performance
Optimized for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and databases
